fields.title: The Boundary
fields.description: Someone's been running product two blocks into Kings territory from the Flats side. The council wants it stopped quietly. They're asking you.

fields.title: The Regular
fields.description: Danny Kowalski hasn't been in for two weeks. Tuesday, Thursday, every week for eleven years, and then nothing. Sal's not asking you to do anything about it. He's just saying it.

fields.title: The Strip Job
fields.description: There's an electronics shop two blocks down from the corner. You've walked past it three times this week without meaning to. Tonight feels like the night.

fields.title: The Tab
fields.description: A guy ran up a tab he couldn't cover and left a flash drive behind the bar. Now two different people are asking about him. Sal wants to know if he needs to worry.

fields.title: The Tail
fields.description: Burner number, no name. Someone's watching a Strip regular and the caller wants to know who hired them. Then they want them gone.

fields.title: The Witness
fields.description: A Kings soldier named Calvin has been missing for four days. His crew thinks he's being leaned on. They want someone outside the Kings to find him before it becomes a loyalty question.

fields.title: The Call
fields.description: Your sister reached out. First time in three years. She needs something taken care of. Family's family.

fields.title: The Morris Job
fields.description: Someone called Morris has a package that needs moving across the Flats. Simple job. First one usually is.

fields.title: Crossing Lines
fields.description: Morris has a delivery that touches more parts of the city than it should. That’s either a mistake or the point.

fields.title: Corky's
fields.description: Dutch wants his dealers back in a bar called Corky's. The owner pushed back. You're the push.

fields.title: The Ride
fields.description: Morris has a courier job. Pick up a package, deliver it across the Flats. Simple work. It stops being simple at the handoff.
